prompt: A WELL project provides short-term rest pods in a designated wellness zone for fatigued occupants. Which Mind concept does this primarily support?

options.text: Stress recovery through access to restorative rest
Water filtration performance
Acoustic zoning for open offices only
Emergency egress capacity calculations

explanation: Brief rest opportunities in calm, private settings help occupants recover from cognitive and emotional fatigue, supporting WELL Mind stress recovery and restoration.

distractorExplanations.b: Water filtration is unrelated to rest pod programming.
distractorExplanations.c: Acoustic zoning may help but rest pods specifically target stress recovery.
distractorExplanations.d: Egress calculations are life-safety code, not wellness restoration.

prompt: Which communication approach best supports WELL Community emergency preparedness during a power outage?

options.text: Multi-channel alerts including visual alarms, text notifications, and audible announcements with accessible formats
A single email sent only to building ownership with no occupant notice
Verbal instructions exclusively, with no written or visual backup
Waiting until occupants self-discover the outage before any communication

explanation: Inclusive emergency communication uses redundant, accessible channels so all occupants—including those with hearing or vision limitations—receive timely instructions.

distractorExplanations.b: Owner-only email leaves occupants uninformed during emergencies.
distractorExplanations.c: Verbal-only communication excludes many users and fails if power affects sound systems.
distractorExplanations.d: Delayed communication increases risk and confusion.

prompt: A community center lobby displays artwork created through a participatory design process with neighborhood residents. Which WELL Community concept does this reflect?

options.text: Civic engagement and inclusive representation in the built environment
Legionella management in cooling towers
Melanopic equivalent daylight calculations
Structural wind load analysis

explanation: Co-created public art fosters community ownership and reflects diverse voices, supporting WELL Community civic engagement and inclusive design values.

distractorExplanations.b: Legionella control is a Water and mechanical operations topic.
distractorExplanations.c: Melanopic daylight is a WELL Light metric.
distractorExplanations.d: Wind loads are structural engineering, not community engagement.

prompt: Which lever-style hardware choice best supports WELL Community universal design at interior doors?

options.text: Lever handles operable with a closed fist and without tight grasping
Small round knobs requiring fine finger dexterity
Recessed pulls mounted above standard reach height
Doors with no hardware, requiring users to push on glass panels only

explanation: Lever handles are a classic universal design solution because they can be operated with limited hand strength or dexterity, unlike round knobs.

distractorExplanations.b: Round knobs require grasping and twisting that many users cannot perform.
distractorExplanations.c: Hardware above reach range excludes wheelchair users.
distractorExplanations.d: Unclear or inaccessible hardware creates operability barriers.

prompt: An employer contracts with a telehealth provider to offer confidential mental health counseling sessions. Which WELL Mind feature category does this address?

options.text: Mental health support and access to professional resources
Active transportation infrastructure
Acoustic privacy between conference rooms
Organic food labeling in cafeterias

explanation: Providing access to counseling through EAP or telehealth services is a direct WELL Mind strategy to connect occupants with professional mental health support.

distractorExplanations.b: Active transportation belongs to WELL Movement.
distractorExplanations.c: Conference room acoustics relate to WELL Sound.
distractorExplanations.d: Food labeling is a WELL Nourishment topic.

prompt: A large hospital uses landmark artwork and distinct floor materials at major intersections to aid navigation. Which WELL Community strategy is this?

options.text: Landmark-based wayfinding to reduce reliance on text alone
Thermal mass optimization
VOC off-gassing documentation
Legionella sampling frequency

explanation: Memorable landmarks and material changes at decision points help diverse users orient themselves, especially when text-based signage is insufficient or unfamiliar.

distractorExplanations.b: Thermal mass relates to building energy and comfort physics.
distractorExplanations.c: VOC documentation is a Materials compliance activity.
distractorExplanations.d: Legionella sampling is a Water management protocol.

prompt: Which altruism program design best aligns with WELL Mind research on prosocial well-being?

options.text: Team-based volunteer projects with choice of cause and recognition of participation at all levels
Forced participation in a single charity with no opt-out
Eliminating all community service references from onboarding
Limiting volunteer activities to unpaid weekend work only

explanation: Voluntary, meaningful volunteer opportunities with employee choice foster intrinsic motivation and well-being benefits associated with WELL Mind altruism features.

distractorExplanations.b: Forced participation undermines voluntary prosocial engagement.
distractorExplanations.c: Hiding service opportunities reduces altruism access.
distractorExplanations.d: Unpaid-only weekend limits exclude many employees from participating.

prompt: A WELL project installs automated external defibrillators (AEDs) in accessible locations with staff training. Which Community concept does this support?

options.text: Emergency preparedness
Nature integration
Healthy food advertising
Circadian lighting design

explanation: AED placement and training prepare the building community to respond to cardiac emergencies, a practical component of WELL Community emergency preparedness.

distractorExplanations.b: Nature integration is a Mind concept.
distractorExplanations.c: Food advertising relates to Nourishment.
distractorExplanations.d: Circadian lighting is under WELL Light.

prompt: Which seating strategy in a public atrium best supports inclusive design for diverse body sizes and mobility needs?

options.text: A mix of bench, armchair, and height-varied seating with adjacent wheelchair spaces
Uniform bar-height stools only with no back support
Fixed stadium seating with no accessible positions
Removal of all seating to discourage lingering

explanation: Inclusive seating provides options for different abilities, sizes, and fatigue levels, allowing equitable use of shared community spaces.

distractorExplanations.b: Bar stools only exclude many users including those needing back support.
distractorExplanations.c: Stadium seating without accessible positions violates inclusive design.
distractorExplanations.d: Removing seating denies rest opportunities for many occupants.

prompt: A workplace installs digital wayfinding kiosks with screen-reader compatibility and adjustable text size. Which WELL Community goals does this advance?

options.text: Wayfinding and inclusive access to navigation information
Moisture intrusion prevention
Smoking cessation counseling
Filter MERV rating selection

explanation: Accessible digital wayfinding ensures people with visual or cognitive disabilities can obtain navigation assistance independently, supporting inclusive community design.

distractorExplanations.b: Moisture prevention is envelope design, not wayfinding.
distractorExplanations.c: Smoking cessation may appear in other wellness programs but not wayfinding kiosks.
distractorExplanations.d: MERV ratings are air filtration specifications.

prompt: Research on biophilic design supports which WELL Mind outcome for office workers?

options.text: Reduced perceived stress and improved attention restoration through contact with nature
Elimination of all HVAC maintenance
Automatic compliance with all Water features
Replacement of emergency drills

explanation: Nature access under WELL Mind draws on evidence that views of nature, plants, and outdoor environments help restore cognitive resources and lower stress.

distractorExplanations.b: Biophilic design does not affect HVAC maintenance schedules.
distractorExplanations.c: Nature elements do not satisfy Water prerequisites.
distractorExplanations.d: Emergency drills remain required regardless of biophilic features.

prompt: A building operator maintains backup power for emergency lighting, fire alarm panels, and mass notification. Which preparedness principle does this follow?

options.text: Critical life-safety and communication systems remain functional during utility failures
All non-essential decorative lighting must stay off permanently
Occupants should evacuate only after power is restored
Backup power is unnecessary if the building has windows

explanation: Emergency preparedness includes resilient systems so occupants can receive warnings and safely navigate during outages, especially in stairwells and assembly areas.

distractorExplanations.b: Permanent shutdown of non-essential lighting is unrelated to backup power strategy.
distractorExplanations.c: Evacuation decisions should not wait for power restoration during certain emergencies.
distractorExplanations.d: Daylight from windows does not replace powered emergency systems.

prompt: Which practice best fosters psychological safety as part of WELL Mind mental health promotion?

options.text: Leadership modeling openness about workload limits and encouraging help-seeking without retaliation
Public ranking of employees by perceived stress tolerance
Penalizing employees who use mental health days
Requiring disclosure of personal trauma in team meetings

explanation: Psychological safety allows occupants to discuss challenges and access support without fear of punishment, a cultural foundation for effective mental health promotion.

distractorExplanations.b: Public stress rankings increase stigma and competition.
distractorExplanations.c: Penalizing mental health days discourages recovery and help-seeking.
distractorExplanations.d: Mandatory trauma disclosure violates privacy and psychological safety.

prompt: A transit-oriented development includes a step-free plaza connecting the building to a public park. Which combination of WELL concepts is most directly supported?

options.text: Community universal design and Mind nature access
Materials VOC limits and Sound reverberation
Water legionella control and Light glare indices only
Nourishment calorie labeling and Thermal PMV

explanation: Step-free connections to public green space simultaneously advance equitable access (universal design) and exposure to natural environments (nature access).

distractorExplanations.b: VOC and reverberation are unrelated to plaza-park connectivity.
distractorExplanations.c: Legionella and glare metrics do not describe accessible nature connections.
distractorExplanations.d: Food labeling and PMV are separate WELL concept areas.

prompt: An office establishes a green team where occupants propose sustainability and wellness improvements. Which WELL Community feature does this represent?

options.text: Civic engagement through occupant participation in shared decisions
Mandatory nature bathing for all visitors
Elimination of all emergency signage
Removal of accessible routes to save space

explanation: Occupant-led committees that influence building policies embody civic engagement by giving users a voice in community-level decisions.

distractorExplanations.b: Mandatory nature bathing is not a standard civic engagement mechanism.
distractorExplanations.c: Removing emergency signage contradicts Community preparedness.
distractorExplanations.d: Removing accessible routes violates universal design.

prompt: Which design of a lactation and wellness room best supports inclusive community amenities?

options.text: Private lockable room with seating, sink, outlet, signage, and proximity to work areas
A shared open kitchen counter with no privacy
A room accessible only by stairs on an upper floor
A storage area with no seating or sanitation

explanation: Dedicated, accessible, private wellness rooms support diverse occupant needs including nursing parents, reflecting WELL Community inclusive design for equitable amenities.

distractorExplanations.b: Open kitchens lack privacy required for lactation.
distractorExplanations.c: Stairs-only access excludes users with mobility limitations.
distractorExplanations.d: Storage areas without amenities do not meet wellness room needs.

prompt: A project limits email notifications after business hours and trains leaders on reasonable deadlines. Which WELL Mind stress factor does this target?

options.text: Chronic work-related stress and burnout risk from always-on culture
Inadequate drinking water availability
Low melanopic light exposure at desks
High reverberation time in open offices

explanation: Boundary-setting around work communication is a organizational stress management tactic that reduces sustained cortisol load and supports recovery outside work hours.

distractorExplanations.b: Water availability is a separate WELL Water concern.
distractorExplanations.c: Melanopic exposure is addressed through lighting design.
distractorExplanations.d: Reverberation is an acoustic issue under WELL Sound.

prompt: Which entry condition best meets WELL Community universal design for a public library?

options.text: Zero-step entrance with automatic door and level threshold at the primary path
Main entrance with 6-inch steps and no ramp
Revolving door as the only entrance option
Accessible entrance located through a remote loading dock

explanation: Equitable access requires that the primary entrance be usable by everyone, including step-free access and doors operable without excessive force.

distractorExplanations.b: Steps at the main entrance create barriers for many users.
distractorExplanations.c: Revolving-only doors exclude wheelchair users and many others.
distractorExplanations.d: Segregated remote entrances stigmatize and inconvenience users with disabilities.

prompt: A corporate campus donates skilled volunteer hours to build affordable housing with a local nonprofit. Which WELL Mind concept is most directly demonstrated?

options.text: Altruism through meaningful community contribution
Thermal adaptability modeling
Acoustic privacy testing
Filter replacement scheduling

explanation: Skills-based volunteering that addresses community needs exemplifies WELL Mind altruism by connecting employees to purposeful prosocial activity.

distractorExplanations.b: Thermal modeling is unrelated to volunteer housing projects.
distractorExplanations.c: Acoustic testing belongs to WELL Sound.
distractorExplanations.d: Filter schedules are HVAC maintenance tasks.

prompt: During wayfinding planning for a senior living facility, which approach best reduces confusion?

options.text: Limit color palette per floor, use large high-contrast type, and place signs before intersections
Use identical finishes on every floor with small low-contrast signage
Rely solely on staff escorts with no environmental cues
Place all directional signs at ceiling level in 8-point font

explanation: Age-friendly wayfinding uses strong visual contrast, floor differentiation, and anticipatory signage placement to support occupants with cognitive or vision changes.

distractorExplanations.b: Identical floors and low-contrast signs increase disorientation.
distractorExplanations.c: Staff-only escorts do not replace environmental wayfinding systems.
distractorExplanations.d: Ceiling-mounted tiny text is difficult for older adults to read.

prompt: Which outdoor amenity best combines WELL Mind nature access with stress recovery?

options.text: A shaded garden with native plants, seating, and walking paths away from traffic noise
A parking lot with no landscaping or seating
A rooftop mechanical yard with locked access
An interior hallway with artificial turf wallpaper only

explanation: Outdoor restorative gardens provide genuine nature contact, comfortable pause points, and separation from stressors like traffic, supporting both nature access and recovery.

distractorExplanations.b: Unlandscaped parking lacks nature and restorative qualities.
distractorExplanations.c: Locked mechanical yards are inaccessible and not restorative.
distractorExplanations.d: Wallpaper imagery does not provide the benefits of real nature access.

prompt: A WELL project documents personalized emergency plans for occupants who need evacuation assistance. Which preparedness principle does this follow?

options.text: Inclusive emergency planning that accounts for diverse mobility and sensory needs
Assuming all occupants can use stairs quickly without assistance
Deferring assistance planning to individual occupants without building coordination
Removing all fire alarms to reduce noise stress

explanation: WELL Community emergency preparedness requires planning for occupants who may need evacuation chairs, visual alerts, or designated assistants, ensuring no one is left behind.

distractorExplanations.b: Universal stair assumptions exclude people with disabilities.
distractorExplanations.c: Uncoordinated individual planning leaves gaps in building-level response.
distractorExplanations.d: Removing alarms creates life-safety hazards.

prompt: Marketing imagery and wayfinding pictograms in a children's hospital represent diverse families and abilities. Which WELL Community principle does this support?

options.text: Inclusive design through representative, welcoming visual communication
Maximum wattage per light fixture
Minimum pipe diameter for condensate drains
Structural live load for file storage

explanation: Inclusive visual communication helps all visitors feel recognized and oriented, reducing anxiety and supporting equitable access to care environments.

distractorExplanations.b: Fixture wattage is a lighting energy specification.
distractorExplanations.c: Pipe diameter is plumbing design, not inclusive imagery.
distractorExplanations.d: Live load is structural engineering unrelated to pictogram diversity.

prompt: An organization measures burnout indicators and adjusts workload policies based on survey results. Which WELL Mind approach does this represent?

options.text: Data-informed mental health promotion and stress management
Elimination of all restorative spaces
Mandatory silence about mental health in surveys
Replacement of counseling with fitness tracking only

explanation: Monitoring psychosocial risk factors and responding with policy changes is an evidence-based WELL Mind strategy for preventing burnout and supporting mental health.

distractorExplanations.b: Removing restorative spaces worsens stress outcomes.
distractorExplanations.c: Silencing mental health topics prevents identifying needs.
distractorExplanations.d: Fitness tracking alone does not address psychological burnout.

prompt: A mixed-income housing project hosts monthly tenant meetings with translation services and childcare during sessions. Which WELL Community features are best supported?

options.text: Civic engagement and inclusive design removing participation barriers
Water submetering and legionella testing only
Circadian melanopic targets and glare control only
VOC product screening and adhesive limits only

explanation: Accessible meetings with language and childcare support enable broad tenant participation in governance, embodying civic engagement and inclusive community design.

distractorExplanations.b: Water submetering does not describe tenant meeting accessibility.
distractorExplanations.c: Lighting metrics are unrelated to meeting participation barriers.
distractorExplanations.d: Materials screening does not address civic engagement programming.
